items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/` **Triggered by:** PR #58408 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed PR #58408 fixed a bug in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` where the top-level `definitions/credential` declared `required: ["CredentialSource"]` — the same property already required by `System.ClientModel`'s schema segment. When the appsettings schema combiner merges segments by definition name, it concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `["CredentialSource", "CredentialSource"]` in the consumer's combined `AppSettingsSchema.json`. The fix removes the duplicate declaration from Azure.Core's schema segment. ## Gaps Found - The `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` is empty — there is no entry documenting this fix. - The `README.md` does not describe the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature at all (it was introduced in 1.52.0), so users encountering the duplicate-`required` issue have no documentation to reference.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — add a `Bugs Fixed` entry under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`. Replace the empty `### Bugs Fixed` section: ```markdown ### Bugs Fixed ``` with: ```markdown ### Bugs Fixed - Fixed `ConfigurationSchema.json` credential definition to remove a duplicate top-level `required: ["CredentialSource"]` entry. When multiple schema segments are merged into `AppSettingsSchema.json`, the combiner concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `["CredentialSource", "CredentialSource"]` in the combined file. Because `System.ClientModel` already asserts this requirement, removing it from Azure.Core's segment eliminates the duplicate with no change in validation behavior. ([#58408](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408)) ``` **File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add a section under `## Key concepts` (after the existing credential bullet) explaining the AppSettings JSON schema feature. Insert the following after the line about `DefaultAzureCredential`/`ManagedIdentityCredential` and before the `### Thread safety` heading: ```markdown ### Configuring clients via `appsettings.json` (IntelliSense schema) Starting with Azure.Core 1.52.0, the NuGet package ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment. When a consumer references Azure.Core (directly or transitively), the build tooling merges these segments into `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. This enables: - **IntelliSense / autocompletion** for `AzureClients` configuration blocks in `appsettings.json` inside Visual Studio and VS Code. - **Validation** of `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and `CredentialSource` options at edit time. No additional setup is required — the schema is automatically merged during `dotnet build`. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new executable snippets are required for this change. The CHANGELOG entry and README prose update above are sufficient. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed beyond the new prose paragraph added in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — verify no unintended README changes. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged. 5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Core] Docs: Azure.Core 1.54.0-beta.1 CHANGELOG missing AppSettings schema bug fix entry
